summary: Analyze clinical datasets for outcomes, utilization, and quality insights with de-identification verification and statistical rigor.

inputs.name: dataset_description
inputs.description: Description of the clinical dataset including variables and population.

inputs.name: deidentification_status
inputs.description: Confirmation that data is de-identified per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination.

outputs.name: analysis_report
outputs.description: Complete analysis with statistical methods and results.

outputs.name: data_quality_assessment
outputs.description: Assessment of data completeness, accuracy, and limitations.

outputs.name: insights_summary
outputs.description: Key findings with confidence levels and clinical relevance.

outputs.name: recommendations
outputs.description: Actionable recommendations based on analysis findings.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is clinical data analysis, not diagnosis or treatment.
Verify de-identification status before any data processing.
Confirm no PHI is present in outputs.
Output must include analysis report, data quality assessment, and insights.

steps.action: Verify data de-identification status and document method used.
Assess data completeness, accuracy, and limitations.
Apply appropriate statistical methods to answer research questions.
Validate results with sensitivity analyses and benchmark comparisons.
Translate findings into actionable quality improvement recommendations.

success_criteria: De-identification status verified before any data access.
Statistical methods are appropriate for the study design and data type.
Data quality limitations are explicitly stated.
Findings include confidence intervals and clinical significance.

rollback.when: If PHI is detected in outputs or analysis methods are inappropriate, roll back and restart with verified inputs.
